plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-289 RP08.P06.M07.S07-RP08.P06.M09.S02 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-289 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP08.P06.M07 test/golden tail (AI retrieval decision binding through leak prevention test), the full M08 Hermes evidence permission cycle (22 rows), and the M09 Claude review packet head (2 rows) across three sections over 40 planned rows with a pack-local row-extras constant, Hermes evidence (H08), Claude review packet (C08), and CP00-290 handoff, without opening runtime permission evaluation, audit event writes, AI runtime dispatch, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-289 is a descriptor-only email/DMS permission-binding slice (RP08.P06.M07.S07-RP08.P06.M09.S02, 40 units: M07 test/golden tail 16, full M08 Hermes evidence permission cycle 22, M09 Claude review packet head 2). All review-focus areas pass. Coverage and deliverable distribution {implementation:12, security_audit:10, ui:8, claude_review:2, test:8} were independently confirmed against registry requirements (registry.js:1671), the slice/coverage validators (validators.js:3888,3923), the test suite (test/model.test.js:1125-1172), and the live closeout-pack-plan (closeout-pack-plan.json:411). Descriptor-only/no-runtime/no-real-data boundaries are sound: no fetch/fs-write/child_process/spawn/SQL/dispatch patterns exist anywhere in packages/email-dms/src, source modules import only registry+service constants, and freezeCp289Result (service.js:2401) stamps writes_product_state:false, evaluates_runtime_permission:false, writes_audit_event:false, loads_real_fixture_data:false, deny_over_allow_enforced:true, and all no-leak flags. The validator script (validate-rp08-email-dms-core-contract.mjs:877) deep-equals the serialized contract projection against the source descriptor, and command-evidence.json records every required validation command at exit 0. Authority boundary is correct throughout: Claude is read-only (tools Read/Grep/Glob), claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, human final approval required, production_ready:false with status implemented_pending_review and explicit no-enterprise-trust-from-local-validation. CP178+ review hardening blockers (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, plus more) are encoded in forbidden_review_evidence (registry.js:490) and the auth preflight passed with valid JSON. Hermes control-plane patterns are used only for deterministic evidence-packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false; no_hermes_runtime_receipt:true), not product runtime. No P0/P1/P2 findings. Two P3 observations are by-design and non-blocking: post-review evidence artifacts are correctly deferred, and a generated scope artifact has a cosmetic classification redundancy. Pack is ready to proceed through the post-review hardening sequence to human final approval.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts referenced by manifest are not yet on disk (expected pre-review state)
findings.details: manifest evidence_refs/required_pack_artifacts (lines 1648-1661) list claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-289/. This is the expected pre-review state: those three artifacts are produced by the hardened review sequence (normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection) that consumes this very review. command-evidence.json notes (line 95) already document these as post-review gated commands executed before commit. Status is implemented_pending_review and pack_level_claude_review.status is pending, so no inconsistency exists at this stage.
findings.recommendation: No action required for this review. At closeout, ensure the post-review sequence actually generates claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json before the commit/goal-closeout gates run.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists the pack's own evidence dir as both implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-289/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 95-98) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 100-105) of this generated scope artifact. The authoritative manifest worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:1695-1698) correctly lists ONLY the v2.0 docx and the LDIP invalid-attempt json, not the pack evidence dir. The pack evidence directory is genuinely part of CP00-289 and must be staged at commit; the broader 'unrelated' list in the generated artifact is a cosmetic redundancy with no effect on the source-of-truth, but a commit script keying off changed-file-scope could mis-skip the dir.
findings.recommendation: Treat the manifest worktree_transition_policy as authoritative for staging; confirm docs/closeout-packs/cp00-289/ is staged (not skipped as 'unrelated') when the pack is committed. Optionally de-duplicate the generated scope classification. Non-blocking.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-289 email dms core permission binding slice descriptor pack
